claim: No Stable, release, conformance, manual assistive-technology, package-parity, or publication claim is made by this source contract.

semantics.nativeBasis: Native panel divs and optional named regions are separated by focusable ARIA separators with sibling native button alternatives.
semantics.name: Every SplitPane.Handle requires aria-label or aria-labelledby by type.
semantics.value: Each separator exposes the preceding panel percentage through aria-valuenow/text and references both adjacent panels through aria-controls.
semantics.landmarks: Panels remain semantic-neutral by default; regionLabel opts a panel into a named region deliberately.
semantics.focusModel: Separators and visible native controls use normal tab order. Narrow sequential mode removes unnecessary handles from layout and focus without reordering panels.

keyboard.behavior: Moves among separators and their visible native controls in DOM order.
For horizontal layouts, moves the boundary spatially by step; physical direction reverses in RTL.
For vertical layouts, moves the boundary by step.
Moves the preceding panel toward its constrained min/max while preserving the adjacent total.
Increases/decreases the preceding panel by two steps.
Collapses/restores collapseTarget when that panel index is declared collapsible.

pointerTouch: Each separator uses pointer capture; visible 44 CSS-pixel decrement/increment and optional collapse/restore buttons provide single-pointer operations without dragging.

state.normalization: At least two non-negative percentages are normalized to a 100 percent total while respecting per-panel min/max and declared zero-size collapsible panels.
state.controlled: value owns layout; onValueChange reports proposals and onValueCommit reports discrete or completed pointer changes.
state.uncontrolled: defaultValue owns initial layout and an optional persistence adapter restores after hydration.
state.persistence: The synchronous adapter is called only in an effect or on committed client interactions; read/write errors are routed to onPersistenceError and never occur during SSR.
state.collapse: Collapsed panels are absent from desktop layout and accessibility exposure, but reappear in narrow sequential mode so a hidden handle cannot strand their content.

responsive.containerQuery: stackAt=narrow switches the layout child at a 36rem root container, not a viewport breakpoint.
responsive.sequentialMode: Panels use DOM order at full width; handles are unnecessary and hidden; all panel content remains available even if a desktop panel was collapsed.
responsive.nested: Every Root establishes its own query container, so nested panes adapt independently.

consumerResponsibilities: Render one Panel for every size index and one indexed Handle between each adjacent pair; keep DOM and visual panel order aligned.
Provide localized separator names and opt into regionLabel only when a panel is important enough to be a landmark.
Use a persistence key scoped to the application layout, validate any external storage implementation, and surface recovery UI when a saved layout matters.
Keep controlled arrays synchronized and revalidate custom constraints, CSS, focus, target sizes, localization, 320-pixel stacking, and RTL.
